articles.title: US Dollar Index: Consolidation below key resistance – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC’s FX Strategist Christopher Wong notes the Dollar Index eased as lower UST yields and softer Oil prices reduced safe-haven demand, with the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) minutes adding no new hawkish impulse.

articles.title: Fed: FOMC minutes back extended hold – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities analysts highlight that April Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) minutes showed growing support to drop the easing bias, with many participants and several regional presidents favoring a more neutral stance.

articles.title: IEA Chief Birol: Oil markets could enter red zone in July-August
articles.expand: International Energy Agency (IEA) Chief Fatih Birol said during the European trading session on Thursday that oil markets could enter "red zone" in July-August as stockpiles deplete and summer demand firms. Birol added, “My hope is that the Strait of Hormuz will open fully and unconditionally.”

articles.title: Indian Rupee gains ground due to oil prices correction, RBI’s intervention
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee (INR) extends its recovery against the US Dollar (USD) on Thursday after underperforming for almost two weeks.

articles.title: Flash PMIs Show War's Impact
articles.expand: There have been no further developments to fuel optimism about the Strait of Hormuz. Polymarket shows little change in the assessment that there is around a 35% chance that the Strait is open by the end of next month and 47% chance opens by the end of July. Still Brent and WTI are trading with a slightly heavier today. Equities are mostly firmer as...

articles.title: Oil: Prices slide on Iran deal hopes – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG notes that the dominant macro driver for Asian markets is the US–Iran conflict and its impact on Strait of Hormuz energy flows, which had pushed global long-bond yields to post-2008 highs and pressured oil-importing Asian currencies.

articles.title: French business activity hits lowest level in more than five years as Oil shock widens
articles.expand: France's private-sector activity takes a deep hit in May, according to preliminary data from S P Global, suggesting that the surge in Oil prices has hit households and businesses hard in the Eurozone's second-largest economy.

articles.title: United Kingdom flash Services PMI unexpectedly declines to 47.9 in May
articles.expand: The United Kingdom (UK) S P Global Composite Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) surprisingly declined in May. The Composite PMI has come in at 48.5;a figure below 50.0 is considered a contraction in the business activity.

articles.title: RBC BlueBay Adds to Yen Longs on Intervention, BOJ Rate View
